Title: Phase separation of MYB73 regulates seed oil biosynthesis in Arabidopsis

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in 12 DAF siliques of MYB73-OE
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced WRI1 expression is associated with lower seed oil biosynthesis in MYB73-OE
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. WRI1 transcript was significantly reduced in MYB73-OE siliques. 2. MYB73 repressed proWRI1 activity in dual-LUC assays. 3. MYB73 directly bound proWRI1 in EMSA.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Preserving WRI1 expression is beneficial for oil improvement; MYB73 repression of WRI1 should be relieved in high-oil engineering.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As a central regulator, perturbation may affect broad seed developmental programs.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although WRI1 is itself a transcriptional regulator, in this study it serves mainly as a downstream target rather than the primary experimental regulator under investigation.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Reduced in 12 DAF siliques of MYB73-OE
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced FAE1 expression is associated with decreased seed oil accumulation and reduced VLCFA-derived fatty acids (lower C20:1 and C22:1; higher relative C18:1 and C18:2 in MYB73-OE)
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Fatty acid elongation / seed oil biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Branch elongation step, downstream of C18 fatty acid production
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses malonyl-CoA with long-chain acyl-CoA substrates during elongation of C18 fatty acyl chains toward C20-C22 very-long-chain fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Elongated 3-ketoacyl-CoA intermediates leading to C20:0, C20:1, and C22:1 fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for VLCFA branch that contributes to seed oil composition
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR; dual-LUC promoter repression assay; EMSA; seed fatty acid profiling in MYB73-OE
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. FAE1 expression was significantly reduced in MYB73-overexpressing siliques. 2. MYB73 repressed proFAE1 activity in dual-LUC assays. 3. MYB73 directly bound the MBSI motif in proFAE1 by EMSA. 4. Altered fatty acid composition in MYB73-OE resembled fae1 loss-of-function effects.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for VLCFA accumulation in seeds
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Maintaining or enhancing FAE1 expression would help preserve VLCFA-containing seed oil traits; avoid activating MYB73 when breeding for high erucic/eicosenoic acid seed oil.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Changes in VLCFA composition can alter total oil composition and quality.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function discussed as major determinant of elongation from 18 to 22 carbons; also known as KCS18.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in 12 DAF siliques of MYB73-OE
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BCCP2 was identified as downregulated in MYB73-OE by RNA-seq and RT-qPCR. 2. MYB73 repressed proBCCP2 activity in dual-LUC assays. 3. MYB73 directly bound proBCCP2 in EMSA.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced BCCP2 expression likely lowers malonyl-CoA supply and contributes to reduced seed oil biosynthesis in MYB73-OE
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Avoid repression of BCCP2 for improving seed oil accumulation; could be stacked with positive regulators of plastid FA synthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulation may affect central carbon flux and seed development.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct catalytic attribution is as ACCase subunit rather than standalone enzyme.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in 12 DAF siliques of MYB73-OE
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for fatty acid synthesis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PKP-β1 expression was reduced in MYB73-OE seeds by RNA-seq and RT-qPCR. 2. MYB73 repressed proPKP-β1 activity in dual-LUC assays.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced PKP-β1 expression likely restricts pyruvate supply for plastid fatty acid synthesis, contributing to lower seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target to preserve carbon flux into seed oil when suppressing negative regulators like MYB73.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Altering pyruvate kinase can broadly affect plastid metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct binding by MYB73 was inferred from promoter repression and motif presence, but EMSA was not shown for PKP-β1 in the main figures.

Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Downregulated in 12 DAF siliques of MYB73-OE
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target storage oil product
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGAT1 transcript level was significantly reduced in MYB73-OE siliques. 2. MYB73 repressed proDGAT1 activity in dual-LUC assays. 3. Discussion states MYB73 directly binds proDGAT1, although direct EMSA evidence was not shown in the main figures provided.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reduced DGAT1 expression likely decreases TAG assembly and seed oil accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for TAG assembly
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Relieving MYB73 repression or boosting DGAT1 may improve seed oil accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May alter seed carbon allocation and storage composition.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct-binding support is weaker in the provided main text than for FAE1/BCCP2/WRI1.

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expression increases during seed development, peaks around 10 DAF, slight reduction, then rises again at 16 DAF; induced by ABA
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed from CaMV 35S in transgenic lines; endogenous transcript responsive to ABA
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S constitutive promoter for MYB73 overexpression in Arabidopsis; CaMV 35S effector in transient assays
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family, subgroup 22; contains two intrinsically disordered regions (IDR1 and IDR2)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression reduces seed oil accumulation, decreases seed size/weight, increases relative C18:1 and C18:2, and decreases C20:1 and C22:1
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses FAE1, BCCP2, PKP-β1, WRI1, and DGAT1 promoter activity; directly binds FAE1, WRI1, and BCCP2 promoters
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-responsive negative regulator that fine-tunes seed oil biosynthesis and VLCFA composition
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Forms phase-separated nuclear condensates; no obligate heteromeric complex partner established
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression associated with phase-separated condensate formation
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Fatty acid biosynthesis, VLCFA elongation, and TAG assembly
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Stable overexpression; seed fatty acid profiling; RT-qPCR; RNA-seq; dual-LUC; EMSA; confocal imaging; FRAP; in vitro phase separation; IDR deletion functional assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced seed size and weight alongside reduced oil accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for genetic manipulation in oilseed crops; repression or editing down of MYB73 may increase seed oil, but seed development and ABA-response tradeoffs should be considered.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB73 expression rises during seed development and is induced by ABA. 2. MYB73 overexpression reduces seed oil content and shifts fatty acid composition away from VLCFAs. 3. MYB73 directly represses FAE1 and also represses BCCP2, PKP-β1, WRI1, and DGAT1. 4. MYB73 forms liquid-like nuclear condensates via IDR1 and IDR2, and this condensation is important for repression activity.
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: ABF2 (upstream regulator of promoter); DNA targets proFAE1; proWRI1; proBCCP2
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Study highlights phase separation as a mechanistic layer for metabolic transcriptional repression.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates MYB73 expression, thereby contributing to negative regulation of seed oil biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly binds ABRE in proMYB73 and activates MYB73 promoter activity
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Upstream ABA-responsive activator of the MYB73 repression cascade
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Indirect control of fatty acid biosynthesis through MYB73 transcriptional cascade
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential upstream lever to modulate oil biosynthesis under ABA/stress contexts; caution because ABF2 is also a stress regulator.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating ABF2 may affect abiotic stress responses and ABA-regulated development.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABF2 activated the MYB73 promoter in transient assays. 2. ABF2 directly bound the ABRE motif in proMYB73 by EMSA. 3. The data support an ABA→ABF2→MYB73 cascade that suppresses oil biosynthetic gene expression.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ABF3 and ABF4 also activated proMYB73 in transient assays, but ABF2 received direct binding validation and is the experimentally established core upstream regulator.
